theme: grow a film into a target landscape
title: Shadowed Film Growth
strategies: trivial: round-robin flux across all columns, oblivious to the target and to the capture/shadowing dynamics (the checker's own reference construction)

greedy (trap): each time step, aim directly at the column with the largest nominal deficit (target - current height), updating an internal model that assumes the shot lands exactly where aimed -- never accounts for shadowing capture, so whenever a tall column sits within the capture radius of a low neighbor, shots aimed at the neighbor keep getting stolen by the tall column, feeding a runaway peak while the valley never moves

strong (insight): at every time step, SIMULATE the true shadowing-capture landing rule for every candidate aim column, pick the aim whose actual (not nominal) landing minimizes the resulting squared error -- pre-compensates for shadowing instead of depositing to shape, so it recognizes and steers budget away from doomed runaway-capture columns
further headroom: multi-step lookahead / global scheduling that jointly orders which columns to fully commit to before shadowing gaps open up, instead of a 1-step simulated choice
